DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Shawn I. Pardo; Callahan, Thompson, Sherman & Caudill, LLP; 949-261-2872; appearing for defendants Pantoll Holdings, LLC, 655-685 4th Street LP, Sutter Street SF, LLC, Cody Chapman and Hans Geiszler.
Jason Wolford; Wolford Wayne LLP; 415-649-6203; appearing for plaintiffs Jonathan Shockley, et al.

Defendants contested the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Moving party failed to show that different theories of liability asserted in Plaintiffs' various causes of action are duplicative warranting their elimination from the operative complaint. The Court notes that, assuming that Defendants are correct that these causes of action are entirely duplicative and add not legal or factual disputes to this action, their elimination will not serve any purpose, e.g. narrowing the issues for discovery or trial.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendants' Cody Chapman, Hans Geiszler, And Sutter Street Sf, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Shawn I. Pardo; Callahan, Thompson, Sherman & Caudill, LLP; 949-261-2872; appearing for defendants Pantoll Holdings, LLC, 655-685 4th Street LP, Sutter Street SF, LLC, Cody Chapman and Hans Geiszler.
Jason Wolford; Wolford Wayne LLP; 415-649-6203; appearing for plaintiffs Jonathan Shockley, et al.

Defendants contested the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part.
Motion is denied as to the request to strike punitive damages. Plaintiffs sufficiently alleges facts to support conscious disregard for Plaintiff's rights.
Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the word "Defendant" in paragraph 9 to be replaced with "Defendants' predecessor-in-interest" as requested in opposition. Plaintiff shall have a 10 day leave to file an Amendment to Complaint limited to this issue.
Motion is denied without leave to amend as to the allegations in paragraph 25 pertaining to the events occurring on or about June 22, 2020 since Plaintiff alleges to have vacated the premises on April 5, 2020.
Motion is denied as to paragraphs 13, 16, 19, 21, 24, 27, 30, 34, 37. Moving party failed to show that these paraphs are false, irrelevant or improper.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
